source.headline: live:sum(shares*price) from portfolio_positions+prices
source.day_dollars: live:sum(shares*price*change_pct)/100 on rows with a live priceRow

metrics.note: unrealized_pct_on_positions is gain on currently-held positions, cost-basis relative. account_lifetime_pct is the 3yr cumulative account performance benchmark. They measure different things.

positions.thesis: Indium phosphide quantum-dot photonic ICs. NASA Phase II + 6 govt R&D contracts. The 1.6T optics bottleneck.
positions.variantPerception: Customer list (NASA, DoD, AI hyperscaler R&D) rerates this when FY27 laser ships

killVectors.body: Execution. FY27 product slip past 2027 or critical NASA program loss.
killVectors.title: Kill vector

positions.thesis: Ethernet backbone for AI, $3.25B AI revenue, cloud switching dominance
positions.variantPerception: Arista is the rare networking name that owns the AI back-end outright rather than riding along on it, and the $3.25B AI revenue line proves hyperscalers are paying for the fabric, not just sampling it.

killVectors.body: Revenue concentration in a few hyperscale cloud buyers means one capex pause hits the order book hard.
killVectors.title: Hyperscaler capex pause

killVectors.body: The multiple has already expanded, so any growth wobble triggers a sharp valuation reset.
killVectors.title: Multiple resets

killVectors.body: Large customers building switching in-house would erode the share the thesis depends on.
killVectors.title: In-house switching

positions.thesis: Arm IP, the chip royalty engine. Every smartphone, growing share of every server.
positions.variantPerception: ARM is the IP layer underneath the chip layer; the position benefits regardless of which custom silicon program wins; near-100% gross margin royalty annuity on the entire non-x86 stack.

killVectors.body: RISC-V hyperscaler adoption | China export ban | AGI CPU channel conflict | Customer concentration + multiple compression
killVectors.title: Kill vector

positions.thesis: Direct-to-cell satellite constellation, AT&T + Verizon agreements
positions.variantPerception: 60 MNO partners is not a thesis, it's a customer book — next 4 quarters convert to revenue

killVectors.body: BlueBird operational failure OR MNO revenue not appearing by Q4 2026.
killVectors.title: Kill vector

positions.note: Frontier sleeve. 15 sh @ 84.06 (-3.6%). AT&T + Verizon agreements. Stop at $70.

positions.thesis: Bitcoin miner with the grid interconnect. The pivot story is using existing interconnect for AI datacenter buildout. The Andromeda site is the proof.
positions.variantPerception: Bitcoin miners with strong grid positions are the cleanest pivot to AI hosting trade in the public market; AI hosting is power-constrained, not compute-constrained.

killVectors.body: Bitcoin mining revenue collapse without pivot | Hyperscaler AI hosting cancel | Power contract renegotiation risk
killVectors.title: Kill vector

positions.thesis: Active electrical cables and SerDes IP for AI clusters. AEC ramp at major hyperscalers.
positions.variantPerception: AECs are a specific answer to a real cabling problem inside GPU clusters, not a general connectivity pitch, which is why Credo's hyperscaler ramp is a purer AI back-end read than the broad connector names.

killVectors.body: Customer concentration at a few hyperscalers means one design loss is a real dent.
killVectors.title: Customer concentration

killVectors.body: The SerDes and AEC lead has to hold against larger, better-resourced connectivity competitors.
killVectors.title: Lead erodes

killVectors.body: The multiple reflects the ramp, so any order softness triggers a valuation reset.
killVectors.title: Multiple resets

positions.thesis: German photonics laser equipment. Niche cap. Recent buy, near breakeven. Thesis is the LIDE process and AI-packaging cross-pollination with the photonic-IC manufacturing stack.
positions.variantPerception: LPKF sits upstream of the optical buildout through the LIDE process and photonic-IC manufacturing, so it captures the packaging cross-pollination tailwind before it reaches the more visible volume optics names.

killVectors.body: A thinly traded micro-cap can swing hard on small flows, which keeps the tape unreadable and the position fragile.
killVectors.title: Thin float liquidity

killVectors.body: A name near breakeven needs the LIDE and AI-packaging story to turn into real recurring equipment orders before the conviction is earned.
killVectors.title: Orders never convert

killVectors.body: Photonics equipment demand is cyclical, so a slip in the optical-IC build delays the cross-pollination thesis.
killVectors.title: Photonics cycle slip

positions.thesis: Fiber infrastructure for hyperscalers. Position is underwater. Thesis: dark-fiber asset base is undervalued and the hyperscaler buildout is the rerate lever. Holding small.
positions.variantPerception: The market prices Lumen as a declining telecom carrier, but the thesis is that it owns the physical long-haul fiber the AI cloud must rent, an undervalued asset base waiting on a hyperscaler dark-fiber deal to surface it.

killVectors.body: The position is already underwater, and the asset value may take years to surface.
killVectors.title: Value stays buried

killVectors.body: The debt load is the live constraint on any turnaround.
killVectors.title: Debt load

killVectors.body: If hyperscaler dark-fiber demand does not materialize, the rerate lever fails.
killVectors.title: Demand no-show

positions.thesis: HBM memory sold out through 2026, AI memory supercycle, most mispriced AI stock
positions.variantPerception: AI-specific HBM cycle, customers pre-paying for capacity that doesn't exist

killVectors.body: Micron, Samsung, SK Hynix all guide HBM oversupplied in same quarter. Today all three guide undersupplied through 2027.
killVectors.title: Kill vector

positions.thesis: GPU cloud infrastructure, $27B Meta deal, 547% growth
positions.variantPerception: Customer-prepayment-funded buildout, $9.3B liquidity, asset-backed financing

killVectors.body: Hyperscaler concentration. Meta or Microsoft pulling capacity in-house. Q1 print did not retire this risk.
killVectors.title: Kill vector

positions.thesis: Optical networking + IP routing. The AI optical pivot is finally printing. Q1 2026 AI/cloud revenue +49% YoY. Optical Networks unit grew 20% in the quarter. Network Infrastructure full-year guidance raised to 12-14% growth. The Infinera acquisition (photonic ICs) is integrating into the portfolio. Holding through the re-rate.
positions.variantPerception: Nokia under ex-Intel-AI CEO Justin Hotard is rapidly transforming into the only Western full-stack vendor combining IP routing + optical transport + silicon photonics + Bell Labs IP.

killVectors.body: Hotard transformation falls flat | Hyperscalers go direct or pick Cisco/Ciena/Coherent | 5G/6G capex slows or shifts
killVectors.title: Kill vector

positions.thesis: Ouster lidar + camera + perception stack for robotics
positions.variantPerception: Digital lidar over analog lidar with structural cost-and-scale advantages; full perception stack shifts from buy-a-part to buy-a-system with stickier margins.

killVectors.body: Robotaxi TAM stagnation | Perception commodity compression | REV8 execution slip | Customer vertical loss
killVectors.title: Kill vector

positions.thesis: AI infrastructure for tier-2 enterprises — every AI data center that is not a hyperscaler. Small position, watch list graduated to live.
positions.variantPerception: While the market prices the hyperscalers, Penguin sells into the non-hyperscaler buildout that is happening right now, a tier-2 demand lane that is underwatched and not a multi-year call option.

killVectors.body: The constructive read rests on a single operator voice while the rest of the room sits neutral, so the conviction is thin and easily unwound.
killVectors.title: Thin conviction

killVectors.body: Tier-2 enterprise AI spend is the softest part of the buildout and the first to pause if capital budgets tighten.
killVectors.title: Capex pause

killVectors.body: Execution risk on selling into a fragmented non-hyperscaler base, where no single contract anchors the thesis.
killVectors.title: Fragmented base

positions.thesis: Second most active launcher after SpaceX, $1.85B backlog
positions.variantPerception: Vertically-integrated space company with Neutron program as inflection point; moves from $50-100M-quarter launch business to multi-billion-dollar-revenue medium-lift competitor.

killVectors.body: Neutron slip | Electron cadence drop | Spacecraft margin compression | Capital raise overhang
killVectors.title: Kill vector

positions.note: Frontier sleeve. 20 sh @ 65.79. Neutron rocket catalyst 2026. Trim 25% at $120, stop at $75.

positions.thesis: Microcap federal cyber/mobility. $218M federal backlog vs $93M mkt cap. Binary catalyst: DHS CWMS 3.0 contract award expected by 2026-06-24.
positions.variantPerception: The market still prices WidePoint like a speculative microcap, but a 218 million dollar federal backlog at 2.3x market cap plus the first profitable quarter since 2021 makes this a real incumbent whose CWMS 3.0 re-award is being underweighted.
killVectors: CWMS 3.0 contract awarded to competitor June 24 2026
Break of 50-day MA $7 for 2 consecutive days
Federal cyber budget cuts hit DHS

positions.thesis: Xanadu quantum computing, photonic-quantum chip with EV Group partnership
positions.variantPerception: Post-dilution gain is the real number; EVG manufacturing partnership is thesis floor

killVectors.body: Second dilution event before manufacturing partnership produces commercial revenue.
killVectors.title: Kill vector

positions.note: Frontier ANCHOR. 250 sh after 4/15 trim (333→250 at $25.22, $2,094 locked). 13.5% of book. Trim ladder: 25 sh at $32 OR 2-day close below $24; 25 sh at $40 or if >15% of book; NEVER full exit.

positions.thesis: Just-IPO'd neocloud (May 2026). $940M long-term contracted revenue at IPO. GPU compute, CPU nodes, managed Kubernetes. Targeting $375M ARR by end of FY26.
positions.variantPerception: The non-obvious point is the contracted book at IPO, $940M in long-term revenue out of the gate is a demand signal most just-public neoclouds cannot show, which de-risks the $375M ARR target more than a fresh listing usually warrants.
positions.note: Pending portfolio_positions sync — surfaced from post-body canonical.

positions.thesis: Brand-new ETF (launched May 6, 2026 on Cboe BZX). Wraps the entire EUV-lithography / semicap-photonics supply chain. 41 holdings, top three TSM, ASML, GLW. 0.35% expense ratio. Spec-sized starter position.
positions.variantPerception: The single-ticker convenience is real, but the diversification is also the ceiling. You will not beat the best holding by owning all of them, and a capex pause hits every name at once.
positions.note: Pending portfolio_positions sync — surfaced from post-body canonical.

positions.thesis: ETF parking — broad-AI-cycle exposure that does not require thinking. Small structural position.
positions.variantPerception: There is no contrarian edge here by design, the single strongest point is that the basket captures the AI compute cycle without forcing a pick among names whose premiums could each reverse.
positions.note: Pending portfolio_positions sync — surfaced from post-body canonical.

positions.thesis: Health-data + AI play. Q1 2026 revenue $348.1M, +36.1% YoY. Pharma partnerships compounding. Position currently -1.17% lifetime. Variant: market reads as precision-oncology lab, real story is AI training-data layer for healthcare.
positions.variantPerception: The market reads Tempus as a precision-oncology lab. I read it as the AI training-data layer for healthcare, with a recurring-revenue pharma business compounding underneath.
positions.note: Pending portfolio_positions sync — surfaced from post-body canonical.

positions.thesis: Recent IPO. AI/HPC colocation + cloud services for GPU workloads. Position currently -5.16% lifetime. Holding through post-IPO chop because the thesis pairs with BRUN at different angle.
positions.variantPerception: The drawdown is post-IPO noise, not a thesis break, the underlying call is that GPU colocation is a real seat in the concrete-and-fiber layer that pairs with BRUN on the same demand wave.
positions.note: Pending portfolio_positions sync — surfaced from post-body canonical.
